designPrinciples: OpenRouter is a provider adapter, not the runtime owner; Convex owns durable jobs, artifacts, leases, traces, and receipts.
Benchmark-shaped work is routed through deterministic tools first, then bounded model edit plans, then evidence-bearing writes.
Free-auto is a long-running/background lane until ladder and p95 evidence prove it can meet interactive collaboration budgets.
Official benchmark claims stay blocked until the external verifier path is wired; internal Convex benchmark readiness is separate.
The scorecard includes every configured agent LLM route from llmModelCatalog.agent plus the curated OpenRouter route set.

cases.title: Durable model-step journaling before replay
cases.acceptance: A duplicate model step replays the recorded result and cannot overwrite the first output hash.

cases.title: Lock/CAS/draft/large-range/resume ladder
cases.acceptance: The latest improvement loop records the collaboration ladder as PASS through L7.

cases.title: Multi-user and human-vs-human coordination proof
cases.acceptance: All recorded multi-user coordination scenarios pass with zero active lock leaks.

cases.title: SpreadsheetBench-shaped tasks route to deterministic tools or bounded model edit plans
requiredConvexContract: deterministic table transforms before model calls
formula edit plans with deterministic formula-result cache
format edit plans with workbook style diff evidence
chart routes require rendered/VLM grade evidence
cases.acceptance: Staged route-selection reports have tasks, and blocked_chart_visual is zero.

cases.title: Rendered chart visual grading lane
requiredConvexContract: Gemini/VLM judge is separate from OpenRouter candidate generation
candidate/gold PNG hashes are recorded before visual acceptance
cases.acceptance: The chart visual probe records chart_visual_grade_proven with an accepted positive candidate and rejected negative control.

cases.title: Agent workspace isolation for benchmark execution
requiredConvexContract: candidate generation sees only agent workspace mounts
evaluator gold is denied until scoring phase
cases.acceptance: Docker probe records container_isolation_proven with evaluator reads denied.

cases.title: BankerToolBench Harbor/MCP/Gandalf verifier path
cases.acceptance: Official BTB contract imports verifier scores from the real Harbor/Gandalf path.
blockers: Record BankerToolBench dataset revision plus a manifest lockfile with per-file hashes.
Run each official task in Harbor/Docker with agent-only workspace mounts before verifier access.
Adapt required MCP financial tools: sec_filings, market_data, company_logo, document_search, web_research.
Import official Gandalf verifier scores instead of using the local smoke verifier.

officialStyleSuites.title: SpreadsheetBench-like workbook edit-plan tasks
officialStyleSuites.acceptance: At least three staged spreadsheet tasks run five repeats with passRate=1, sidecar evidence, local formula cache, p95 latency, and zero contamination.

officialStyleSuites.title: BankerToolBench-like package/verifier workflow
officialStyleSuites.acceptance: A staged BTB-like task emits the expected deliverable package from agent-only files before evaluator metadata opens and local weighted scoring reaches 1.0.

officialStyleSuites.title: Multi-user and agent conflict tasks
officialStyleSuites.acceptance: Human-vs-human, agent-vs-human, blocked lock, stale base, and finally-release scenarios all end with conflict data and zero lock leaks.

officialStyleSuites.title: Provider route N=5/p95 path stability
officialStyleSuites.acceptance: Every route promoted for interactive benchmark-shaped writes has N>=5 live managed-path evidence with p95 model/tool calls, fingerprints, and zero invalid or missing tool results.
blockers: 34 agent route(s) still need N=5/p95 live path evidence before interactive promotion.

routePlans.label: Nex AGI Nex-N2-Pro free
requiredContract: agentJobs is the durable root for every benchmark run, including /ask and /free
Convex actions call providers; browser/client_action never calls OpenRouter directly
agentStepJournal records model input/output hashes before replay can skip duplicate provider calls
agentOperationEvents counts query/action/mutation/model/tool/scheduler steps
agentLeases fence slices and prevent duplicate workers from committing the same job
mutationReceipts tie every canonical cell write to a job, tool call, baseVersion, and result
artifact ids remain the system of record; provider file/cache ids are adapter metadata only
CAS conflicts return as data, not thrown errors, so OpenRouter models can re-read and recover
blockers: route needs N>=5 p95 ladder evidence before interactive promotion
